name: A. J. Abercrombie, Jr.
description: Abercrombie is a rather easy going sort of fellow, and one that all like. He doesn't strut around and advertise just where he is going, but you can depend on it that he gets there.

name: Dovey Fair Anderson
description: If pluck and determination to win out at all costs means anything in life, we know that Dovey will win by a large margin. Coach Dillon will miss this plucky back when the 1928 season starts.

name: Zack Yeargin Appleton
description: Who doesn't know Zack? He is one of Howard's popular ministerial students, and he is a true Bulldog supporter. He has been right there with his colors on and his vocal chords tuned every time the Bulldogs met an opponent. He is also a prominent student and unbroken success is predicted for him.

name: Frank R. Awbrey
description: Awbrey's voice and genial personality have made him a mainstay of the glee clubs, and his presence next year will be sorely missed. He is not only an outstanding student in things musical, but takes an active interest in all things collegiate.

name: Baker, W. Morgan
description: Baker is one of those fellows who takes life seriously, and if he is going to do a thing you can depend on it that he is going at it whole-heartedly. A free thinker and a profound student, he has become an outstanding figure on the campus during the last two years, after dropping out a year when he was half through and taking up his abode in Florida.

name: Bancroft, William Henry
description: Our vocabulary is simply too limited to say what we would like to about Billy. Howard has probably never had a greater athlete, nor will ever have one, who, with his athletic prowess, has a more pleasing personality, is more thoroughly a gentleman, a more conscientious student than is he. He was born a leader, and he demonstrated his ability as a general when he led the Crimson Bulldogs through the past season, as captain of the freshman team, as president of the Freshman Class, and on many occasions when he was looked upon unofficially, but without question, as the boss. Bancroft is a News scholarship man, and the News should be and is proud of him.

name: Barger, Guilliard
description: Just to look at Guilliard creates the subconscious thought that there must be fairies after all, but to hear her play is to perpetuate belief in the tiny folk. Ah, and so petite! Maybe that's why she loves to "parlez-vous." Anyway, she has done her work thoroughly at Howard, and you may expect her to make good from now on.

name: Battles, James Leonard
description: Battles is one of our most popular ministerial students—one who has shown his interest in every phase of college life, including politics. As president of the Ministerial Association this year he has done fine work.

name: Edward E. Beason
description: Ed went to Alabama for two years and then decided to get his diploma at the best school in the world. Although he hasn't spent very much time on the campus, due to his insurance "interests," he has won himself a place in everyone's heart.

name: Clifton G. Brown
description: "Cliff" is not only an outstanding man "footballically" speaking, but in every line of endeavor. He buys his clothes at Blach's—therefore, dresses well and succeeds whether it be with coeds, professors, upper classmen, or just mere rats. He is one of Howard's most outstanding men in the athletic firmament, and withal is a most likable boy—really, he is just a grown-up boy.

name: Jewel Burnham
clubs: Etowah County W. M. U. Scholarship
description: Jewel has the uncanny knack of making the Honor Roll consistently, and keeps on friendly terms with teachers and students alike. But "uncanny" is the wrong word. "Perseverant" is better, for there is not a more conscientious student on the campus. She has also taken a prominent part in Women's Student Government.

name: Murray S. Cawthorne
description: We have seen very little of Murray during his sojourn at Howard (maybe that is because we don't go to the Science Hall often enough), but in reading the life lines in his hands we find that success awaits him without.

name: Horton B. Chamlee, A.B.
description: Chamlee strayed away from Howard for a year's work at Alabama, but we are glad to have him come back and graduate with the good old Class of '28. He is a good student and is liked by the whole student body. If he makes as good a doctor as he is a friend, the world will be a better place to live in.

name: Margaret Davidson, A.B.
description: Margaret is another language shark. She can "polly-voo Frawnsay" or "Ah, blah, Espanyol" with the best of them, and we look for her to wind up in the language department of some college before long. Books have not taken all her time, however, and she has found occasion to take part in many student activities.

name: Roy E. Deas, A.B.
description: Deas has spent the greater part of his time in the Science Building, but he has found time to cultivate many friendships. He is an all-round good fellow and the professors can vouch for the fact that he is a good student. We'll hear more of Roy.

name: Owen Dees, A.B.
description: Owen is a true artist. Although he does not wield a brush and palette, he can shoulder his trusty camera and tripod and find beauty in the most common places. Most of the views in this year's annual are results of his eye for the beautiful. The camera is not his only weapon, however, because he has a mean right arm that sees service every spring with the baseball team.

name: ARTHUR DIX
clubs: State Executive Council Y. M. C. A. (3)
description: Dix is a sheik of no mean ability, but the remarkable part of it is that he can still meet his classes on time the next morning. His string of honors bespeaks his varied interests in all student activities. If you are looking for a real "go-getter," Arthur is your man.

name: MILTON E. DOUGHTY
description: Being a product of Newton Institute no doubt has helped "Doc" to make good. Quiet and unassuming, we find Doughty a most accommodating student—and when it comes to tooting that trumpet he is right there!

name: ERNEST L. EPLEY
description: Epley probably knows more about French and Spanish than any other student in school. He is majoring in French and taking the most advanced courses in Spanish, so he ought to be well versed. He is one of the really deep thinkers at Howard and his well-founded views have won him many friends. Howard's loss will be gain for the world at large.

name: WILLIAM LESLIE EZELL
description: Ezell is one of the outstanding ministerial students at the college, being liberal in his views and a profound student of the Bible. He will do a great service to the world in the work he expects to follow.

name: Ferry, James Adolph
description: Although his name is apt to suggest a "slow method of transportation over a narrow stream," there are no slow methods found in this "Ferry." He is quick, alert, and well informed over a large field. Howard will miss him in '29.

name: Franks, Bessie Ledbetter
description: Howard won't be the same without Mrs. Franks. She has won friends in all her classes, and is known as a foremost student. She has been a helpful friend to many students, and although the school hates to see her leave, we as a class are proud to have her finish with us this spring.

name: Gibson, John Drue
description: Who doesn't know "Hoot"—the clown of the campus and a student who has not an enemy in the whole college. Everybody likes "Hoot," and "Hoot" likes everybody. He is another one of those Walker County athletes, and we hope that said county has a lot more fellows like him to send our Alma Mater. Gibson started out on the road to fame as an end on the Bulldog eleven, but sustained a broken ankle. He got his letter this year, however, and well deserved it.

name: Grant, Ralph D.
clubs: State Winner of Atwater Kent Radio Contest
State Representative in Southern District Radio Contest
description: Ralph is another of the song birds and one whose merit as a songster has been recognized on many occasions during his college life. As soloist with the Glee Club this year he made a great hit on the trip. Looking into the crystal we see a bright future for him in all things musical. Good luck!

name: Thelma Grogan
description: "Studies" seems to have been Thelma's hobby while at Howard—a thing which many a student might wish to have set down permanently as a reminder of their efforts—also a thing which others might have said of them if they had been such a hard-working and conscientious student as Thelma. Her thoughtfulness of others makes her a friend indeed.

name: Earl Raymond Hammett
description: Hammett is one of those boys you just can't keep down. Besides carrying a heavy course in college, he has worked more than the law allows. A genial fellow is Earl.

name: Helen Hardie
clubs: Y. W. C. A. Undergraduate Representative
description: Howard has never known a more loyal student than Helen. She is willing to work day and night for the glory and betterment of her Alma Mater. No doubt that is the reason she has been added to the faculty. Success is sure to crown her efforts in any field of endeavor.

name: Sue Undine Harris
description: The dictionary does not carry the name "Undine," but in this case at least we are sure it means "hard working," for Sue has certainly shown her worth in every line of endeavor at Howard. Her place will be hard to fill.

name: Louise Hawkins
description: A modest, retiring young lady of fine capabilities and an unceasing worker. Studying has been her hobby—a mighty fine one to have. By the way, she is majoring in French, and her thesis books have come all the way from France itself. In those of us who have no linguistic ability, her knowledge of French inspired profound awe. Louise has remained behind the screens, always ready to do her bit for the college we love.

name: Joe Heacock, Jr.
description: Joe is not only a saxophonist de luxe, but is the sort of student that we like to see at Howard. He is interested in anything for the betterment of Howard and is willing to work to that end.

name: Mildred Hearn
description: How Mildred could graduate in three years and take such a prominent part in student activities at the same time is more than we can fathom, but the fact remains that she did it. And we're mighty glad to have her graduate with us. She is one of the most attractive little blondes on the campus, and is very popular. She was our historian.

name: Kathryn Hendricks
description: Maybe "Kat" is due an apology, since her name is not in the correct alphabetical order, but you see she didn't let us in on the secret that she had a brand-new name. Although Kathryn (we haven't learned to say "Mrs. Saxon" yet) hasn't been with us the whole four years, she has been here long enough to become a real Howardite. Much luck to you and Herman—may joy and happiness be yours.

name: Rupert S. Hicks, A.B.
description: The treasurer of the student body this year has worked hard, and we know that he well deserves his salary (?). Aside from a few week-end trips to Judson (!) Rupert has stayed right on the job. Big business is calling to him now.

name: Lucile Higgins, A.B.
clubs: U. R. of Y. W. C. A. (3)
description: "Shorty" is one of Howard's most popular co-eds. Petite is she, but when she wants to put something over you might as well fall in line. The "love bug" seems to have bitten her ferociously this year, but we're for you, "Shorty"!

name: Robert Holbrook, Jr., A.B.
description: Robert hails from the house of Holbrook, famous for its Big "Snipe" and Little "Snipe." He is whole-hearted and earnest in his every endeavor, and although quiet at times, he readily makes a good impression.

name: J. Harold Hood, A.B.
description: Harold possesses rare friendliness, and may always be found with a smile on his face. He is a Howard booster out-and-out, and wherever Harold goes the fame of Howard is sure to go. His amiability will win a way in the world.

name: Arthur L. Horton
description: "A. L." is a real student of the Bible and we predict for him a big service in the pulpit. He is a true Howard supporter, and takes a great interest in all things, especially those of a religious nature. He and his wife have made many friends at Howard.

name: Ruby Horton
description: Ruby is one of the biggest religious workers of our class. She has taken an active part in all B. S. U. and Student Mission activities, and we look for her to continue her good work after she gets her diploma this spring.

name: Mamie Howard Houston
description: Mamie has a loyal cognomen and, true to her name, she is a most untiring booster for Howard. A hard worker and a conscientious student is most fittingly said of Mamie Howard. To say that she was elected to Pi Kappa Tau gives the record of her scholarship.

name: Sara Hunt
clubs: Secretary of Y. W. C. A. (2)
Music Committee of Y. W. C. A. (4)
description: Just a wee bit of humanity, but a pianist of no mean note. Sara plays differently from all others—she plays without the begging. As accompanist for the Glee Clubs, she has made good when others couldn't. Her sunny disposition will be missed at Howard next year.

name: James Theodore Jackson, A.B.
clubs: President College B. Y. P. U.
Representative State Y. M. C. A. Council (4)
Voted Most Unusual Student and Hardest Working Boy at Howard (4)
description: "Little Jack," though young in years, has shown his right to be the world's youngest student body president; Howard's highest ranking scholar; Howard's most representative student, etc. No one doubts Jack's ability in anything. His place will be hard to fill next year. Good luck to you, Jack!

name: Helen Johnston, B.S.
description: Helen certainly knows her Chemistry, and everything else that she has taken. Her hard work and amiable nature have endeared her to all of the students and faculty. Dr. Sampey will have to look long for an abler assistant. She has the distinction of being the first co-ed to receive the Honors Degree from Howard.

name: Henry Hendon Jones, B.S.
description: If you've ever seen Henry Jones standing still, you've got something on us. He's a dynamic sort of fellow, full of plots and schemes, which he has the ability to carry through. Since the first day he set foot on the Howard campus he has been working for the betterment of the college, and has helped greatly in student activities of every nature.

name: Lewis E. Kelley, A.B.
clubs: Representative to Faculty and Chairman Pulpit Committee (3, 4)
description: Kelley has spent most of his time around the Ministerial Club, so all of us have not had a chance to come in close contact with him. He has, however, been one of the most sincere religious workers on the campus since coming to Howard, and has a bright future before him.

name: WHITNEY J. KING, JR.
description: "Whit" is quite a ladies' man and has also played in the band. He has played on the varsity basketball team and keep: "guard" over a certain member of the co-ed basketball team. A likeable boy that casts the present aside for the future is no other than he.

name: HENRY CLAY KNIGHT
description: Clay is easily the busiest student at Howard, both on and off the campus. As director of the Sponsor Club he has done some fine work for Howard students. This Knight is rightly called the "Sheik of Arabia." He is the capable manager of this year's Annual.

name: WILLIAM GEORGE LAATSCH
description: George is a remarkably quiet boy and one that is only on the campus because he means business. We are sorry that we haven't seen more of him during his stay at Howard. You may be sure it will be done if you "let George do it."

name: WILLIAM HENRY LANGLEY
description: William is a Howardite first, last and always. He is ever on the alert to do some good turn for the dear ole school that he loves so well. This year he has shown that a vice-president can work if he wishes to.

name: Nancy Lea
description: Nancy has gone about her work quietly, but with this same quietness has won her way into the hearts of many students. Such a persevering worker is bound to succeed in life.

name: S. A. Lecroy
description: Lecroy is another student whose influence is felt rather than heard, for he never makes a bluster over things he is doing—he just does them silently and competently. He is a credit to the institution.

name: Bernard Lee
description: Where have you kept yourself these four years, Bernard? Why, we hardly know you, and yet we do know that you are going to be a great surgeon, for the hard work you are doing must eventually bring success your way. We wish you lots of luck.

name: Marion Lee
description: "Banker" has a way of his own that is unique to say the least, and it gets results. This year "Banker" has gotten down to real work, and "there's a difference." May he live up to his name and save lots of money and then give it to Howard.

name: HUGH M. C. LINDER
clubs: Member Executive Committee of Scientific Society
description: We know that Hugh's ambition to be a great surgeon will be realized, because he does not confine his activities to dreaming. He spends hours upon hours in the laboratory in preparation for higher study, and is one of the best pre-med students at Howard. His appointment to a place as laboratory instructor is one proof of his ability. Science Hall has claimed him most of the time, but he has found time to take part in many activities.

name: GEORGE J. LITTLE
description: "Shorty" joined the "draft" department of a local bank soon after coming to Howard, and ever since then he has been up and going. Even though outside duties require much of his time, he has nevertheless, taken a somewhat active part in the various college activities.

name: OLER D. MASON
description: Mason has made a good record as a student and in the meantime has found time to make many friends, along with his scholarship. We prophesy for him a career of unbroken usefulness.

name: WILLIAM THOMAS MAYNOR
description: "Tommy" is a newspaper man that can make the old typewriter sing out wonderful stories of Howard College and its many activities. As a member of the fourth estate, "Tommy" has done a great deal to make Howard known to all the world. He has been invaluable in getting up the ENTRE NOUS this year in more than his specific work of athletics. He will enter the newspaper field with a sure enough collegiate background.

name: Hugh McClendon, A.B.
description: Hugh is a quiet sort of fellow who spends most of his time off the campus, but his stately bearing and sensible conversation are qualities that have made him stand out. "Mac" is one student who is the same, no matter what crowd he happens to be in at the moment. He's a credit to our class.

name: James Brown McClendon, B.S.
motto: "Do your own work if you can't find somebody to do it for you,"
description: Brown's happy-go-lucky, devil-may-care manner has made him known to everybody. "Do your own work if you can't find somebody to do it for you," is his philosophy, and we find him in the thick of things in every class. Although he has not stayed on the campus, he has made many friends among the students.

name: Emory Speer McNider, A.B.
description: As an active worker in the religious work of the college, Emory has no peer. For four years now he has really worked hard and has made friends by the score. He is a studious, whole-hearted boy, who has left his mark on the campus without any noise.

name: Annie Lee Orme, A.B.
description: Judson claimed Annie Lee for two years, but the urge to come to Howard was too great and to Howard she came. She is a girl of fine ideals and a winning disposition that can't be beaten. Howard hates to see her leave.

name: Passmore, Harry, Jr.
description: Science Hall won't look the same next year, because Harry has become a permanent fixture down there in the "lab." He's a true scientist and sticks to his work like an Edison or a Watt. Being president of the Howard Scientific Society is only one of the many honors achieved by his hard work.

name: Payne, Lowell G.
description: Payne has been a pillar and post in all student activities at Howard. A staunch supporter of all things of interest to the student body and to the college as a whole, we cannot say too much to praise him. He is one of our outstanding ministerial students and one of the comparatively few real thinkers on the campus. A steady friend—and a man.

name: Pence, Marguerite Louise
clubs: Secretary Chi Delta Phi National Honorary Fraternity (4)
President Beta Pi Theta National Honorary Fraternity (3, 4)
description: Marguerite can do more things and do them better in the least amount of time than anyone in college, and if there were any more honors to be attained, "Six" Pence would attain them. She is system personified, and this, no doubt, accounts for her remarkable efficiency in every line of endeavor. As editor-in-chief of this year's Entre Nous she has demonstrated her journalistic ability in a fine way. It's a trite expression, maybe, but we hate to imagine Howard without her.

name: Phillips, Loyal
clubs: President of this year's Senior Class
description: Loyal certainly lives up to his name when it comes to showing loyalty to Howard (and, incidentally, to Evelyn). He is a boy of remarkable vision, a scholarly mind, and a sincere friend to all students. He is a boy that is always full of pep, and whenever he starts a thing he puts a "snap" into it that carries it over. As president of this year's Senior Class Loyal has made good. In all student activities he has ever made good.

name: William Richard Porter, A.B.
description: "Bill" is another of those working collegians. He has worked soon and late since coming to Howard, and he rightly deserves his "dip." What will the co-eds do for specials when Porter is gone? May his work in the future bring as much joy to his fellowmen as he has brought in envelopes to his fellow students.

name: Alyne Price, A.B.
description: Last fall Alyne went to Cuba with the football team, but she came back the same friendly little girl that she has been ever since the day she entered Howard. A girl of high ideals like Alyne is sure to make her mark in the world.

name: Everett Lane Rhodes, A.B.
description: "Dusty" Rhodes is a philosophical sort of fellow who looks upon the roses and thorns of life with the same cynical smile. While he has little to say except when spoken to, his quiet and reserved manner has made many friends for him.

name: Pace W. Roberts, Jr., B.S.
description: "Red" is as fiery and energetic as his flaming curls indicate. He's always doing something, and he does nothing uselessly. He toots a mean trombone, and his hard work as manager of the band has been one of the most important things that kept the organization going. His experience as a salesman has caused him to be appointed on almost everything that has collecting in it. We can't appreciate "Red" too much.

name: Stanley Brooks Russell, A.B.
description: S-h-h! S-h-h! There comes Howard's quietest ed. Though rather shy to some, Brooks has won his way into the hearts and minds of many students. He is quiet and unassuming, but when he speaks you can depend on what he says, and his infrequent smile is well worth waiting for. His "Time and Tide" in the Crimson has been perused with interest this year.

name: Evelyn Sellers, A.B.
clubs: Secretary Beta Pi Theta Honorary French Fraternity (4)
description: Evelyn is the other co-ed who made the trip to Cuba last fall. Her first year at Howard was enough to capture our hearts, and being the "wonderful girl of our dreams," she has managed to hold them all so far intact. She is a sure enough princess, not only in looks but in every way, and the underclasses hate to have her bid them adieu.

name: James Clarence Sellers, A.B.
description: Sellers is one of Howard's most versatile preachers. He holds down a heavy course and does lots of active church work at the same time. He is outspoken in his convictions and a prominent member of the Ministerial Association. Incidentally, "Red" was a star in the preachers' line in the game with the "Infidels."

name: Ruth F. Sherer, A.B.
motto: "I do not choose to write myself up."
description: Ruth has been in Howard and out of Howard, having tried another school one year, but of course she found Howard better and came back. She is the capable senior editor of the Entre Nous this year, but she says, "I do not choose to write myself up." Strictly speaking, Ruth has been associate editor, for she has had her finger in on nearly every page. We know she will make good in the future.

name: ALLEN ANDERSON SIMPSON
description: Allen is for all business, fun and pleasure. Since entering upon his collegiate career he has found time to work, study and become a factor in many college activities. Like his buddy, "Oscar" Phillips, he is a Howard booster of no mean repute. This year, as business manager of the Crimson, he has demonstrated what a real business man he is going to be.

name: APSEY P. SMITH
description: Apsey has probably been late at more classes than any one at Howard, but when he does arrive he misses nothing that takes place. He has not been seen on the campus so very much during his sojourn here, but those who have come to know him vouch for his being a most likeable chap.

name: JAMES LEMUEL McKINLEY SMITH
description: If there is anything in Birmingham that Jimmy Smith has not held an office in, it must be the Association for the Blind. His dominating personality has made him a prominent figure in the city and on the campus, and lots of shrinking freshmen will confide to you that for the first six weeks they thought he was a member of the faculty. He is one of the strongest links that connects Howard with the outside world.

name: EDMUND MARVIN SMITH
description: "Bull" had hard luck this year in the realm of football by being hurt in the first game of the season, but where the team went his spirit went also. Although a football player of the first calibre, "Bull" has spent much time in the laboratory among the frogs, etc. Just the kind of a friend you like to have is "Bull" Smith.

name: Robert E. Smith
description: Robert has not spent much time on campus courses and consequently is not so well known as he might have been, but we find him a young man of sterling worth and one that is sure to make a way for himself in the world.

name: Margaret Henrietta Staples
description: Margaret has only been with us for two years now, and Howard hates to give her up so early. She is the first girl to be "tapped" for Hypatia during her first year on the campus, but there's a reason. A harder worker cannot be found and to one of her ability and intellect, success is sure to come.

name: David R. Stevens
description: David is fond of his mathematics and has taken practically all that the college offers. He has been campus shy during his four years here, but those who know him have come to appreciate his real worth.

name: Grace Stuckey
description: Grace loves religious work, and we know that if she follows her first inclination it will be towards this line of work, in which she is bound to succeed. She is a quiet sensible girl, well worth knowing.

name: Taylor, Eva Eunavee
description: Eva is intensely earnest in everything she undertakes and sincerely works at anything she is called upon to do. In spite of her unusually quiet reserve, she has numerous friends who appreciate her real worth.

name: Turnipseed, Mabel C.
description: Mabel has the happy faculty of being able to learn foreign languages as easily as Helen Johnston has been able to learn chemistry. She is a girl with a smile that wins and a sincerity of purpose and devotion to the right that cannot be questioned. Howard could certainly use several more Mabels next year\u2014and every year.

name: Weaver, Edgar Edwin
description: E. E. Weaver is a true Howardite. He has been an outspoken supporter in everything for the betterment of the school and of the class, and when in the future we shall look back and count over the real friends we had in the Class of '28, one of the first we'll think of will be Mr. Weaver. He is one of the mainstays of the Ministerial Association. We like him a whole lot.

name: Wilking, John Lowman
description: Johnny is another of our famously valiant Bulldogs, who has for the last time donned his uniform for the Crimson and Blue. This young man is witty and no mistake. When he is around the moments seem to take wings and make their flight. It is rumored that \"Pictorial Reviews\" are already coming addressed to Mrs. \"Johnny.\" We wonder!

name: Arthur B. Windsor
description: Arthur is a true student of the Bible, and we look for him to do much beneficial work when he leaves college. He is little seen on the campus, but in his classes he ranks with the best.

name: Cecil S. Wood
description: Big, jovial Cecil is found in the midst of things. His booming laugh is known to every student, and Howard will lose one of her best liked ministers this spring. He takes an active part in student matters, and played a good game in the Preacher-Infidel contest. He also does active church work.

name: Walter O. Woolley
description: Otis comes from down Judson way, and we wonder just how he managed to leave the old home town, which holds so many attractions, but anyway we are glad that he came to Howard. He is one of the best-hearted students that we know, and is a good, steady scholar. Best wishes, Otis.

name: Helen French Wright
description: "Heck" left us for a year, but returned this year to receive her degree from Howard. Courted by some and loved by all—that's "Heck," who is all Wright. Our college days have been brighter because of her cheery disposition. If a smile goes a long way, then Helen is sure of success.

name: W. Russell Bickley, Jr.
description: Bickley is the boy with the provocative Southern drawl that you read about in novels. He is not hasty with words, nor does he act without sufficient deliberation (for instance, he was the last senior to have his picture made), but he claims your attention when he does speak and act. We cherish his friendship.

name: Henry Eugene Long
description: The Entre Nous would not have been complete without "Pop." He has been a factor in school these four years, finding time to go out for football every year, make his grades and even raise a family, too. It is safe to say that every student on the campus knows "Pop" and feels that he deserves his "H." Although he didn't see a great deal of service, still he gave his all every time he was sent on the gridiron.

name: Byron B. Nelson
description: "Blondy" is one of the most energetic fellows on the campus, and never has an idle moment. He is a three-year graduate, therefore the Class of '28 claims him, but the Class of '27 didn't want to give him up, so they have his picture, too. Besides keeping up a heavy course, he finds time to exhibit his superiority in tennis, and Hoyle himself can't juggle the cards any better in a bridge game.

name: Margaret Thornton
description: Margaret started her college career in Florida, but a better school soon claimed her. Again this year she strayed away to another school for awhile, but the call to Howard was overpowering, and now we are fortunate to have her step in again and get her sheepskin with the Class of '28. We like her so well that we are really jealous of any school that kept her away from us.

name: Codie D. Bell
clubs: State Student Council Y. M. C. A. (1)

name: Chrystine Clyburn Bomar
description: Judson College (1); Voted Neatest Girl (1); Voted Best Dressed Co-ed (3).

name: Evelyn Giles
clubs: Beta Pi Theta National French Fraternity (3)

name: Maurine Ethel Gipson
clubs: Treasurer Woman's Student Government, Junior Representative Woman's Student Government (3)

name: JOHN ROBERT RUSHING
clubs: President College B. Y. P. U. (2)
Delegate Y. M. C. A. Milwaukee Conference (2)

name: SARAH HOOVER
clubs: Social Chairman Y. W. C. A.
